scope: wave2 migration: 3 of legacy's 4 read streams (guilds, channels, roles) are ported at Tier 1; the members stream is out of scope for this migration (ENGINE_GAP — see docs.md's Known limits) and keeps its legacy implementation. Discord's source surface here is read-only (Capabilities.Write is false), so there are no write endpoints in or out of scope.

excluded.reason: ENGINE_GAP: the after=highest-user-id cursor is derived from the nested user.id field of the last record in the page, but the engine's cursor pagination last_record_field reads only a flat top-level key on the raw last record (paginate.go's lastRecordFieldValue: last[field]), with no dotted-path support. Discord's real Guild Member object has no top-level id (docs.discord.com/developers/resources/guild), only a nested user.id, so this exact legacy pagination shape cannot be expressed in streams.json without a Tier-2 RecordHook/StreamHook, which this wave is forbidden from authoring. Blocked; legacy internal/connectors/discord keeps serving this stream until a follow-up wave adds either dotted-path last_record_field support or a hooks/discord package.
Pass B capability expansion; not modeled by legacy discord connector
Pass B capability expansion; not modeled by legacy discord connector
Pass B capability expansion; a mutation, not part of this read-only migration
